title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for rabbitmq 4.3 retries, priorities, and consumer timeouts and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go client and broker-policy compatibility record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feature Version Gate
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 2226 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry, strict priority, and consumer-timeout policy limits; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 behavior on deployed broker version, queue type, policy, feature state, amqp091-go behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feature Version Gate.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for gate rabbitmq 4.3 behavior on deployed broker version, queue type, policy, feature state, amqp091-go behavior, migration state, and tested fallback. For RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feature Version Gate, keep Feature Version Gate connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Delayed Retry Policy, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Delayed retry is enabled by default for every quorum queue in RabbitMQ 4.3. In Go, a new broker policy can be assumed safe for existing Go consumers without a compatibility drill.

options.text: Declare RabbitMQ and Go outcome, topology, ownership, identity, and byte, time, attempt, and concurrency budgets for the quiz Delayed Retry Policy case

Write the quiz evidence note for Feature Version Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 1651 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry, strict priority, and consumer-timeout policy limits; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 behavior on deployed broker version, queue type, policy, feature state, amqp091-go behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feature Version Gate, keep Feature Version Gate connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 behavior on deployed broker version, queue type, policy, feature state, amqp091-go behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.
